steps.title: Add the equations to eliminate y
steps.content:
The two equations have $y$ terms with opposite coefficients. Adding them eliminates $y$:
$$(3x + y) + (4x - y) = 13 + 15$$$$7x = 28$$

steps.title: Solve for x and determine the number of solutions
steps.content: Dividing both sides by 7 gives $x = 4$. A unique value for $x$ indicates the two lines intersect at exactly one point, so the system has exactly one solution. That matches choice A — A is correct.
